display.purpose: Hero headlines on the landing. One per page.

headline.purpose: Section headings on landing surfaces; primary page titles in product.

title.purpose: Card titles, modal headings, in-page sub-sections.

body.purpose: Default reading text. 65–75ch line length in long-form output.

label.purpose: Kicker labels, metadata, status pills. UPPERCASE, IBM Plex Mono, 0.18em letter-spacing.

shadows.name: ambient-soft
shadows.purpose: The only sanctioned shadow. Used under floating cards inside graphic compositions and on raised hover states. Warm-tinted because the rgba base is Workshop Black, not pure black.

motion.name: ease-out-expo
motion.purpose: The system's default entrance curve. Used for the Build Map path-draw, card lift-ins, and any orchestrated reveal.

motion.name: ease-out-quart
motion.purpose: Slightly softer alternative to ease-out-expo. Use when the entrance should feel less decisive.

motion.name: ease-out-quint
motion.purpose: Snappier alternative. Use for state-change feedback (button press, focus shift).

motion.name: duration-state
motion.purpose: Default state-change duration (hover, focus, color shift). Used across inputs and buttons.

motion.name: duration-entrance
motion.purpose: Card / element entrance (Build Map cards, etc.).

motion.name: duration-orchestration
motion.purpose: Total length of a one-shot orchestrated entrance like the Build Map. Reserve for hero-moment graphics.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Default commitment button. Action Red, used wherever the user is asked to commit.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 40px; padding: 10px 20px; background: #DC2626; color: #FFFFFF; border: none; border-radius: 6px; font-family: 'Sora', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: -0.005em; cursor: pointer; transition: background 200ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1), transform 200ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #B91C1C; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.98); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #DC2626; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Secondary commitment. Used alongside Primary in dual-CTA layouts.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 40px; padding: 10px 20px; background: #FFFFFF; color: #1C1917; border: 1px solid #EAE0D8; border-radius: 6px; font-family: 'Sora', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: -0.005em; cursor: pointer; transition: background 200ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: rgba(245, 240, 235, 0.5); } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #DC2626; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Tertiary text-action. Used in navigation and within-content actions.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 36px; padding: 8px 14px; background: transparent; color: #8A8480; border: none; border-radius: 6px; font-family: 'Sora', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: background 200ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1), color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: rgba(245, 240, 235, 0.5); color: #1C1917; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Default text field. Quiet at rest, red-tinted glow on focus.
components.css: .ds-input { display: block; width: 100%; height: 44px; padding: 10px 16px; background: #FFFFFF; color: #1C1917; border: 1px solid #E8DDD5; border-radius: 12px; font-family: 'Sora', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; transition: background 200ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1), border-color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1), box-shadow 200ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #4A4040; } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: rgba(220, 38, 38, 0.3); background: rgba(220, 38, 38, 0.02); box-shadow: 0 0 0 4px rgba(220, 38, 38, 0.15); } .ds-input:disabled { opacity: 0.4; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Product Card
components.description: Default product surface card. Rounded corners, hairline border, used inside the dashboard.
components.html:
Project Alpha

Last edited 2 hours ago.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #FFFFFF; border: 1px solid #EAE0D8; border-radius: 8px; padding: 24px; font-family: 'Sora', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-card__title { margin: 0; font-size: 18px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: -0.02em; color: #1C1917; } .ds-card__body { margin: 8px 0 0; font-size: 14px; color: #4A4040; }

components.name: Landing Card
components.description: Sharp-cornered card for landing-page composition. Declarative voice.
components.html:
Feature

Research, Fast

Get competitor context in minutes, not weeks.
components.css: .ds-card-landing { background: #FFFFFF; border: 1px solid #EAE0D8; border-radius: 0; padding: 28px; font-family: 'Sora', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-card-landing__kicker { display: inline-block; font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.18em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #8A8480; margin-bottom: 18px; } .ds-card-landing__title { margin: 0; font-size: 24px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: -0.03em; color: #1C1917; } .ds-card-landing__body { margin: 16px 0 0; font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.6; color: #4A4040; }

components.name: Pill Label
components.description: Mono kicker / metadata pill. Wherever the system says 'this is metadata.'
components.css: .ds-pill { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 6px 12px; background: #FFFFFF; border: 1px solid #EAE0D8; border-radius: 9999px; font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.18em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #4A4040; }

components.name: Sticky Nav
components.description: Landing-page sticky header. White-translucent backdrop with a faint blur for legibility.
components.html:
Idea2App
FeaturesPricing
components.css: .ds-nav { position: sticky; top: 0; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; height: 64px; padding: 0 32px; background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.95); backdrop-filter: blur(4px); border-bottom: 1px solid #EAE0D8; font-family: 'Sora', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-nav__brand { font-size: 18px; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.01em; color: #1C1917; text-decoration: none; } .ds-nav__links { display: flex; gap: 32px; } .ds-nav__links a { font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; color: #1C1917; text-decoration: none; transition: color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1); } .ds-nav__links a:hover { color: #4A4040; } .ds-nav__cta { height: 40px; padding: 0 24px; background: #FFFFFF; color: #1C1917; border: 1px solid #EAE0D8; border-radius: 6px; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; cursor: pointer; }

narrative.overview:
A working space, not a showroom. Tools laid out where the hand reaches for them, paper marked up in red, a single confident accent against tinted white. The system reads as utilitarian and unhurried at the same time. It speaks like a peer who has shipped things, not a vendor selling things.

The atmosphere is quieter than the Linear / Vercel lane and closer to Stripe's measured calm. Tinted-white surfaces carry the weight. The red accent appears at moments of action, progress, and emphasis, never as decoration. Typography does the heavy lifting: scale contrast, weight contrast, careful tracking.

The system explicitly rejects the generic AI-tool aesthetic, the stock SaaS template, the crypto / web3 look, and editorial-magazine drift. If a screenshot could be confidently labeled as any of those, it has failed.
keyCharacteristics: Tinted-white surfaces, never #FFF flat. Background #FAFAFA; warm-paper #F5F0EB for muted secondaries.
One decisive accent (Action Red #DC2626), used on ≤10% of any given screen.
Sharp corners on landing-page composition; gently rounded corners (8px) inside the product workspace.
Strong scale contrast in typography. Flat hierarchies are the tell of an undesigned page.
Flat by default. Shadows appear only as ambient elevation under floating elements; never as decoration.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Action Red appears on at most 10% of any given screen. Its rarity is the point. If you find yourself tinting two unrelated elements red in the same composition, one of them is wrong.

rules.name: The Tinted Neutral Rule
rules.body: Pure #000 and pure #FFF are forbidden. Every neutral carries a warm tilt (Workshop Black, Cloud, Warm Paper, Card White). The warmth is barely visible at full lightness and structurally important at scale.

rules.name: The Auxiliary Accent Rule
rules.body: Warm Coral, Warm Sand, Warm Ember exist for product-internal communication (status, dependency, advisory). They do not appear on the landing, in marketing copy, or in the brand mark.

rules.name: The Tight Tracking Rule
rules.body: Display and Headline tracking is -0.06em, not flat zero. Hero typography with default tracking reads as a wireframe. The tightening makes large type feel intentional.

rules.name: The Mono-Label Rule
rules.body: Metadata, status, and kicker labels are IBM Plex Mono UPPERCASE with letter-spacing 0.18em. Not Sora small caps, not Sora at smaller weight. The mono shift is the system's way of saying 'this is metadata, not voice.'

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. Shadows appear only under elements that are not part of the document flow (overlays, floating cards inside SVG compositions). Never on a card that's just sitting in a list.

rules.name: The No Glassmorphism Rule
rules.body: backdrop-filter: blur() is forbidden as a decorative treatment. The only approved use is a faint header backdrop on the sticky landing nav, which is a legibility tool, not a style.

dos: Do use Action Red (#DC2626) only at moments of action, progress, or emphasis. The One Voice Rule is non-negotiable.
Do tint every neutral toward Workshop Black. Never #000, never #FFF.
Do respect the corner-system split: sharp on landing, gently rounded inside the product.
Do use IBM Plex Mono UPPERCASE with letter-spacing 0.18em for kicker labels, status pills, and metadata.
Do ease out with cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1) (ease-out-expo) on entrance animation. Match this curve across new motion.
Do honor prefers-reduced-motion by showing the final static state. Every entrance animation needs a non-animated fallback.
Do cap body line length at 65–75ch in long-form output (PRDs, reports, tech specs).
Do use the Build Map's idioms (ease-out-expo, ambient soft shadow, mono kicker label) when introducing new signature graphics.
donts: Don't use border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text over a gradient). Solid colors only. Emphasis through weight or size.
Don't ship a 'Powered by AI' badge, sparkle emojis, or a purple-blue gradient hero. Generic AI-tool aesthetic stays out.
Don't ship a 3-column rounded-card grid of icon + heading + paragraph. The stock SaaS template look stays out.
Don't default to dark mode. The product is a workshop, lit. Dark is reserved for the sidebar.
Don't drift into editorial-magazine territory: no Cormorant, Playfair Display, or italic drop caps on a builder tool.
Don't use bounce or elastic easing curves. Ease-out-expo / quart / quint only.
Don't animate layout properties (width, height, top, left). Use transform and opacity only.
Don't nest cards. If your design calls for a card-in-card, one of them is wrong.
Don't introduce a fourth font family. The Space Grotesk / Sora / IBM Plex Mono trio carries the whole system.
Don't use em dashes in copy. Use commas, colons, semicolons, periods, or parentheses.
Don't add accent inflation. Warm Coral / Warm Sand / Warm Ember are auxiliary; they don't appear in marketing or in the brand mark.
